audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Luke prologue, Spirit promise, ascension, witness to ends of earth, upper room prayer, Judas/Scripture, Matthias.

verses.restored: The first account I made, O Theophilus, concerning all that Jesus began both to do and to teach,
until the day when, having given commands through holy Spirit to the apostles whom he had chosen, he was taken up.
To these he also presented himself alive after his suffering by many proofs, appearing to them through forty days and speaking the things concerning the reign of the Divine.
Gathered with them, he commanded them not to depart from Jerusalem, but to wait for the promise of the Father, which, he said, you heard from me.
For John baptized in water, but you will be baptized in holy Spirit not many days from now.
Those therefore who had come together asked him, Lord, are you at this time restoring the reign to Israel?
He said to them, It is not yours to know times or seasons that the Father set in his own authority.
But you will receive power when the holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and as far as the end of the earth.
Saying these things, while they were beholding, he was lifted up, and a cloud took him from their eyes.
As they were gazing into heaven while he was going, look, two men stood by them in white clothing,
who also said, Men of Galilee, why do you stand looking into heaven? This Jesus, taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way as you beheld him going into heaven.
Then they returned to Jerusalem from the mountain called Olivet, which is near Jerusalem, a Sabbath day's journey away.

When they entered, they went up into the upper room where they were remaining: Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James of Alphaeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas of James.
All these were persevering with one accord in prayer, together with women, Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
In those days Peter stood up in the midst of the siblings, and the crowd of names was about one hundred twenty, and said,
Men, siblings, it was necessary for the Scripture to be fulfilled, which the holy Spirit spoke beforehand through David's mouth concerning Judas, who became guide to those who seized Jesus.
For he was numbered among us and received the allotment of this service.
This one therefore acquired a field from the wage of injustice; falling headlong, he burst open in the middle, and all his inward parts poured out.
It became known to all those dwelling in Jerusalem, so that the field was called in their own language Akeldama, that is, Field of Blood.
For it has been written in the scroll of Psalms: Let his dwelling become deserted, and let there be no one dwelling in it; and, Let another take his overseer-service.
Therefore it is necessary that of the men who accompanied us during all the time the Lord Jesus went in and went out among us,
beginning from the baptism of John until the day he was taken up from us, one of these should become a witness with us of his resurrection.
They put forward two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also called Justus, and Matthias.
Praying, they said, You, Lord, knower of all hearts, show which one of these two you have chosen
to take the place of this service and apostleship, from which Judas turned aside to go to his own place.
They gave lots for them, and the lot fell to Matthias, and he was counted together with the eleven apostles.
